title: Listen and Choose a Response — Extracurricular

questions.stem: Are the basketball courts open for free play during lunch hours?

options.A: Yes, they're open from noon to 2 PM for anyone with a student ID.
options.B: Basketball is a popular sport on campus.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about facility availability, so providing the schedule and access requirements directly answers their question.

questions.stem: There's a Korean film festival at the student center this Friday. Want to go?

options.A: That sounds great! What time does it start?
options.B: Korean films are very popular internationally.
options.C: I watched a Korean movie last weekend.

questions.explanation: The speaker is inviting you to an event, so showing enthusiasm and asking for details is the natural accepting response.

questions.stem: Can students use the music practice rooms even if they're not music majors?

options.A: Yes, they're open to all students. You just need to reserve a time slot.
options.B: Music majors have priority for practice rooms.
options.C: I took piano lessons when I was young.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about access policies, so clarifying the policy and reservation process is helpful.

questions.stem: I'm so bored this weekend. There's nothing to do on campus.

options.A: The student union is hosting a game night on Saturday. You should check it out!
options.B: Weekends are for resting and catching up on sleep.
options.C: I usually go home on weekends.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing boredom, so suggesting a specific campus activity is a constructive and helpful response.

questions.stem: I want to get involved in community service. Are there any student organizations for that?

options.A: Definitely! The Community Outreach Club meets every Tuesday. I can introduce you.
options.B: Community service is required for some scholarships.
options.C: I volunteered at a shelter last semester.

questions.explanation: The speaker is looking for volunteering options, so recommending a specific organization and offering to help connect is very helpful.

questions.stem: The spring music festival was canceled due to budget cuts. I'm so disappointed.

options.A: That's really unfortunate. Maybe the student council can organize a smaller alternative.
options.B: Music festivals require a lot of planning.
options.C: I didn't go to last year's festival.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing disappointment about a canceled event, so sharing their feelings and suggesting a possible alternative is supportive.

questions.stem: Is there a membership fee to join the photography club?

options.A: It's only 10 dollars per semester, which covers film and darkroom access.
options.B: Photography clubs are a great way to learn new skills.
options.C: I used to be in the photography club.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about costs, so providing the specific fee and what it covers directly answers their question.

questions.stem: I'm thinking about trying out for the campus theater production, but I'm shy.

options.A: You should go for it! Everyone is nervous at first, but it's a lot of fun.
options.B: Theater productions require a lot of rehearsals.
options.C: I prefer watching plays to performing.

questions.explanation: The speaker is hesitant about trying something new, so offering encouragement and normalizing their feelings is supportive.

audioSegments.text: Are the basketball courts open for free play during lunch hours?
There's a Korean film festival at the student center this Friday. Want to go?
Can students use the music practice rooms even if they're not music majors?
I'm so bored this weekend. There's nothing to do on campus.
